* Add 5 missing LaTeX files: FPE step renumbering and SU3 derivation sequence

FPE_verification/:

  from step7; FPE reference updated to Step 7; table corrected to show
  non-commutativity/ordering results at step 7
- step7_noncommutativity.tex: Non-Commutativity in FPE (Gap G3), renumbered
  from step5; title updated to Step 7

SU3_derivation/:
- step1_generators_from_8D.tex: Direct 8D map C⊗H → Gell-Mann (SKETCH,
  negative result); companion script tools/verify_su3_from_biquaternion.py
- step2_octonion_embedding.tex: SU(3) via G₂ = Aut(O) restriction (SKETCH,
  auxiliary); SU(3) = Stab_{G₂}(e₇) acting on C³ ≅ span{e₄,e₅,e₆}
- step3_SU3_result.tex: Final verdict PROVED [L0]; three-approach comparison
  table; confinement = Clay Millennium Problem; DERIVATION_INDEX update

All files use CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 header and \ifdefined\INCLUDEMODE pattern.
All 5 files compile successfully with pdflatex.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Gap A analysis files and Step 7 weight motivation

Create five new LaTeX files:
- gap_A_resolution/A1_modular_Lfunction.tex: tests L(1,f), L(2,f), and
  Dedekind zeta values for the Step-6 newforms; viable candidate is
  L(2, f_{k=4}) at N=38 (Conjecture A1)
- gap_A_resolution/A2_modular_curve.tex: tests vol(X_0(N))/pi against
  B_phenom; finds near-coincidence vol(X_0(137))/pi = 46 within 0.65%
- gap_A_resolution/A3_two_circle.tex: two-circle KK sum on T^2(R_t,R_psi);
  concludes DEAD END — large-ratio limit gives only B_0=8pi, small-ratio
  requires compact real time violating Lorentz invariance
- gap_A_resolution/A4_hecke_connection.tex: most promising approach; tests
  Petersson norms, period integrals, conductor arithmetic; open candidate
  is L(2, f_{38}^{(4)}) via Conjecture 4.1
- three_generations/step7_weight_motivation.tex: derives k=2n weight-
  generation correspondence from psi-winding angular momentum and SL(2,Z)
  modular transformation of complex time; extends to k=8 fourth generation

All files use CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 header and \ifdefined\INCLUDEMODE pattern.

commit.message:
Replace Error.isError() with instanceof Error checks (#277)

* fix: replace Error.isError with instanceof Error for browser compatibility

Error.isError is a TC39 proposal not yet available in all environments,
causing TypeError crashes on Chrome Mobile iOS and other older browsers.
Replaced all 10 occurrences across client and server files with
`instanceof Error` which is universally supported.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CzG9cv4nJtxWW9fsxzZYTa

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: gemini-code-assist[bot] <176961590+gemini-code-assist[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert backend changes

commit.message:
fix: Coming Up Next was always empty due to playlist IPC failure

_jukebox_fast_get 'playlist' piped mpv's response through
'jq -r .data // empty', which pretty-printed the entire playlist
array as 1000+ lines of JSON. For large playlists (263+ tracks,
~38KB), socat -t 0.5 would timeout before receiving the full
response, resulting in empty pl_json and always showing
'End of playlist'.

Fix: bypass _jukebox_fast_get entirely for the playlist property.
Instead, make a single socat call with -t 2 (longer timeout) and
extract the next filename + item ID directly from the raw mpv
response via jq '.data[$idx]?.filename', avoiding the intermediate
pretty-printed variable altogether.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add sccache to CI workflow for faster Rust builds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement git commands as MCP tools (#34)

Implement Epic 4 — Git Commands Implementation. Adds the git command
runner and tool builder that converts command config entries into
registered MCP tools.

New modules:
- src/git/runner.ts — spawns git processes with timeout and output capture
- src/git/tools.ts — converts CommandConfig to ToolDefinition with Zod
  input schemas, runtime guard validation, and CLI arg mapping
- src/git/index.ts — barrel exports

All 7 configured commands are now functional MCP tools:
- git_status: show working tree status
- git_log: show commit logs (configurable limit)
- git_diff: show diffs (staged, unstaged, between refs)
- git_branch: list branches (local or all)
- git_show: show commit or object by ref
- git_blame: show line attribution for a file
- git_remote: show remote repositories

Updated src/index.ts to wire config loading → tool building → registry.

Tests: 31 new tests (runner + tools), all 90 pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(blazor): fix WebSocket corruption from response compression + CSS error

Root cause: UseResponseCompression() wrapped the response stream BEFORE
MapRazorComponents/MapHub, corrupting WebSocket frames. Circuit connected
but immediately crashed: Server returned an error on close.

Fix: UseWhen(!IsWebSocketRequest) skips compression for WebSocket upgrades.

Also: removed nonexistent GoldAIG.Web.styles.css ref (MIME error), fixed
deprecated meta tag, enabled SignalR EnableDetailedErrors in non-prod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Test262 25% pass rate milestone (closes #254) (#379)

Wire builtins to the test262 runner's global environment by calling
install_globals(), include assert.js by default per the Test262 spec,
add function property side-table so that ad-hoc properties on Function
values (e.g. assert.sameValue) work correctly, support PlainObject
constructors in the Construct opcode, expand the unsupported-features
skip list for features the engine does not yet implement, run each test
on a dedicated thread to isolate stack overflows, lower
MAX_CALL_STACK_DEPTH to 512 to prevent native stack exhaustion, and
add clear_call_stack() for test harness cleanup.

Raise the CI test262 threshold from 0.0% to 25.0%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

all pre-existing local DB jobs to be re-appended to the sheet as new rows.

commit.message:
Update 1.Enrichment/1.TRANSACTION.es.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add missing environment variables to hosted agent container

The container was crashing immediately on startup with:
  System.InvalidOperationException: AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID environment variable is required

The azd agent extension auto-injects AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME,
and STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, but the app also requires AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID,
RULES_STORAGE_URL, and AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT which were not being passed through.

Add environment_variables section to azure.yaml container config using envsubst
syntax to resolve values from azd environment outputs at deploy time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Show welcome message at least five times before suppressing (#48)

* Initial plan

* Show welcome message at least five times instead of once

Replace the single marker file approach with a counter-based approach
in both 00-remix.sh and 00-remix.fish. The marker file now stores a
count that is incremented each time the message is shown. The message
is suppressed only after it has been displayed 5 times.

commit.message:
fix(actions): make deletePortfolio atomic by catching P2003 FK error

The previous count-then-delete pattern had a TOCTOU race: a transaction
could be inserted after the count and before the delete, causing the DB
to throw a generic error instead of the user-facing message.

Fix: attempt the delete directly and map Prisma P2003 (foreign key
constraint violation) to the friendly 'has transactions' message.
All other errors still surface as generic failures.

Updated tests to mock the FK error path rather than the count mock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use unique coverage filenames per test project (#32)

## Problem

All test projects were writing their coverage output to the same file
(`coverage.cobertura.xml`), causing each project to overwrite the
previous one's report.

## Solution

- Removed `--coverage-output coverage.cobertura.xml` from the `dotnet
test` command so that the test runner auto-generates a unique
(GUID-based) filename per project.
- Updated the ReportGenerator reports glob from
`**/coverage/**/coverage.cobertura.xml` to
`**/coverage/**/*.cobertura.xml` to pick up all uniquely-named coverage
files.

commit.message:
Add TLS module ID limit to prevent unbounded growth

Implements IMPROVEMENT_FINDINGS.md section 6.2 (No Limits on Module ID Growth).

Changes:
- Add maxModuleID constant (65536) to cap the number of TLS modules
- Check module ID limit before incrementing in RegisterModule()
- Returns clear error if limit exceeded

This prevents:
- Unbounded memory growth in the DTV (Dynamic Thread Vector)
- Module ID overflow on 32-bit systems
- Memory waste with huge module IDs used as array indices

Impact: Minimal complexity increase (5.7 overall, well below threshold of 10)
Tests: All tests pass (go test ./...)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add move animations using Showdown client FX sprites

Add pokemon-showdown-client as submodule for FX sprite assets.
Create animation engine with projectile, contact, beam, AoE, and
self-target animation styles. Each of the ~40 moves has a config
defining its effect sprite, trajectory, background flash, and shake.
BattleScene now runs async animations per log entry instead of
simple CSS class toggles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 5 new metrics: route dispatch, WAL read, UI render, serialization, GC stats (#1066) (#1070)

Add subsystem timer metrics (route dispatch, WAL read, UI render,
serialization) and GC statistics (gen0/1/2 collections, total allocated
bytes) to the metrics infrastructure.

- Extend IMetricsTracker with 5 new Record* methods
- Add 12 new fields to MetricsSnapshot record struct
- Implement thread-safe tracking in MetricsTracker with Interlocked ops
- Instrument BareMetalWebServer route dispatch with Stopwatch timing
- Add static callbacks on BmwJsonWriter, WalDataProvider, HtmlRenderer
- Wire up callbacks in BareMetalWebExtensions.InitializeAsync
- Add subsystem timers and GC statistics sections to metrics table
- Update all mock/stub IMetricsTracker implementations in tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ac): explicit tool steps in skills + synthesis nudge after writes

ac-architect: numbered ÉTAPES with explicit tool calls
  1. list_files  2. code_write(INCEPTION.md)  3. output full content
  Forbid src/ writes without INCEPTION.md first

ac-codex: numbered ÉTAPES with TDD order
  1. code_read(INCEPTION.md)  2. code_write(tests)  3. code_write(impl)

executor.py: inject synthesis prompt when _written_count > 0 AND output < 80 chars
  Asks agent to show full content of written files (min 200 words)
  Compute count inline from all_tool_calls to avoid F821

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
